channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: The 2026 World Cup, France's most successful football manager's swan song
item.description: He is France's most successful football manager, and is looking to win a second World Cup with his team for his last year as the French national coach. But who is Didier Deschamps? FRANCE 24's Aurore Cloé Dupuis went to ask Deschamps' friends and former coach.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Europe under red alert amid exceptional early heatwave
item.description: It's been less than a month since a May heatwave set records across several European countries. Now the heat is back and temperatures across the continent may raise even higher in coming days. According to the WHO, 200,000 people across the EU have died due to heat since 2022.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French heatwave drives up demand for AC, despite concerns
item.description: The second early-season heatwave of the year has brought the return of a politically-charged issue. That of air-conditioning - for decades, France has been notoriously reticent to adopt the technology - currently just 25% of French households have it. That figure is up significantly from just a decade ago, and it's set to keep rising as climate change brings increasingly frequent episodes of extreme heat. France's largely nuclear-based power generation means that the increased electricity consumption of AC has less of an environmental impact than elsewhere - but there are other concerns.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham's, the UK's likely next prime minister?
item.description: Britain's prime minister is expected to lay out a timetable for his departure. Keir Starmer has been under pressure to go since last month's dismal election outcome for the Labour Party. The man vying the country's top job is the Mayor of Greater Manchester, who's just secured his return to parliament. But who exactly is Andy Burnham?
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Ballet brings me so much joy and I want to spread it': Kadeem En Pointe
item.description: A male ballet dancer is on a mission to encourage more male dancers to take up the more traditional female role of one who goes up on his or her toes, or en pointe. Kadeem En Pointe has filmed himself for the first time here in Paris dancing in front of the city's major monuments. He spoke to us about his ongoing campaign in Perspective.
item.dc:creator: Stuart NORVAL

item.title: For Iraqi minorities, World Cup representation is a source of pride
item.description: France faces Iraq in a group stage match on June 22. The Iraqi team faces a significant disadvantage in this challenging group. But even after a 4-1 loss to Norway in its first match, for the country, this World Cup appearance remains an event not to be missed following a 40 year absence from the competition. This is especially true in Kurdistan in the north of the country where the presence of players from national minorities is significant.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French government holds crisis meeting over sweltering heatwave
item.description: France is set to experience the hottest day yet in its latest heatwave after a sweltering night, with a peak of 43C expected in the southwest. Forecasters warn the extreme weather episode could end up being as serious as a 2003 heatwave that claimed the lives of nearly 15,000 people nationwide. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar takes a look at the French government's recommendations as it holds its latest crisis meeting.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump-backed candidate wins Colombian election
item.description: A fiercely divided Colombia has chosen a new president in a razor-thin runoff that’s already sparking tension across the country. While some celebrate the victory of right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella, others are protesting what they see as a dangerous shift to the right.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'JD Vance put lipstick on a pig: Iranians have the upper hand ' in peace negotiations
item.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of American Studies and Int'l Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. According to Lucas, the US is not negotiating with Iran from a position of strength: "The Americans shifted from regime change to regime surrender", he explains, "trying to bomb their way to get the Iranians to capitulate on a number of conditions. That failed." So as the US ruled out ground troops, they had to come "back to the table". And Lucas said the US really miscalculated with respect to: Iran's retaliation on the Gulf states and the impact the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would have on the global economy.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: What do Iranians think of the Iran-US deal?
item.description: As talks continue between the US and Iran, our colleagues at France 2 got access to rare on-the-ground perspectives in Tehran. Anti-regime residents had hoped that the war would bring change. But with Iranian leaders seemingly more unified than ever, their disappointment is hitting hard.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Maternity leave in India offers progress, with mixed results
item.description: In 2025, the Supreme Court of India declared maternity leave a fundamental right for working women. India is among the countries in the world where pregnant women are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave, which can be taken for up to two children. The move has been widely welcomed by women's rights advocates. But they emphasise that India still has a long way to go in ensuring the rights of working mothers. FRANCE 24's Navodita Kumari and Zubair Dar report.
item.dc:creator: Navodita KUMARI

item.title: Change of UK PM likely 'a passing of the crown' while early general election unlikely
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party but would remain caretaker prime minister until a new head is chosen by the party. His likely successor is Andy Burnham, who won the Makerfield byelection a few days earlier. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle looks at the likely scenarios for the UK.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Cape Verde stun again, Spain get their redemption
item.description: Spain got their World Cup redemption and their first win by comfortably beating Saudi Arabia 4-0. Belgium went down to 10 men after the eighth red card of the tournament was handed out. The Red Devils struggled against Iran, and that game ended on a dry tally of 0-0. The surprise of the evening played out in Miami, as Cape Verde managed to draw against Uruguay. Egypt finally got their hands on their first World Cup win as the Pharaohs won 3-1 against New Zealand. Plus, France get ready for their game against Iraq this Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: Former US Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
item.description: Alan Greenspan – the longtime US Federal Reserve chief who served under four presidents – has died at 100, his wife said in a statement on Monday. Greenspan presided over an unprecedented period of American economic expansion but was later faulted for failing to rein in markets ahead of the 2008 global financial crisis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Norway’s ‘Viking row’ takes over Times Square
item.description: Norway fans took their signature ‘Viking row’ to New York’s Times Square on Sunday on the eve of their World Cup clash with Senegal. Since it was performed in Boston ahead of Norway’s World Cup opener last week, the “row” has gone viral and has been seen on escalators, boats and even in the Norwegian parliament.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Anatomy of a fall: Keir Starmer, from election landslide to precipitous descent
item.description: Dutiful rather than dynamic, Keir Starmer was elected to be a safe pair of hands after years of chaos under Britain’s Conservatives. His term ended less than two years later amid corruption allegations, policy U-turns, and after links were revealed between his choice for US ambassador and Jeffrey Epstein, a man Starmer never met.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Replay: JD Vance addresses US-Iran talks
item.description: Rewatch as JD Vance addresses talks between US and Iran in Switzerland to put an end to the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Yamal boosts Spain as Belgium held to draw again
item.description: After a disappointing opening draw against Cape Verde, Spain comfortably brushed aside Saudi Arabia (4-0), powered by Lamine Yamal's first-ever World Cup goal and an Oyarzabal brace.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: 'Doomchessing' is the new digital addiction, as users flock to Chess.com
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, June 22: Papers discuss the expected departure of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. In France, the brutal heatwave didn't stop the massive street party, Fête de la Musique. Also: AI-generated influencers are flooding social media. Finally, can online chess mania replace doomscrolling?
item.dc:creator: Elitsa GADEVA

item.title: France struggles to deal with new heatwave amid punishing temperatures
item.description: The head of the French state-owned railway operator warned incidents could not be ruled out due to the high temperatures. The heatwave is having a strong impact on the rail network and ​can deform tracks and damage overhead electrical lines. This comes as many schools have also been forced to closed down. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Race starts to replace Keir Starmer as UK PM, with Burnham as favourite
item.description: UK is about to get his seventh prime minister since the Brexit vote after UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on June 22nd. The favourite is Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester who's win in the Makerfield byelection increased pressure on Starmer. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: As Cuba's power crisis deepens, Havana's residents speak out
item.description: Under pressure from the Trump administration, the Cuban government has announced a sweeping package of economic reforms — a historic opening, marking the gradual liberalisation of the communist regime. But the island is still running on empty: electricity rationed to a trickle, and chaos still the order of the day. An exclusive report from Havana.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Iran's first round of talks concludes despite Trump threatening strikes
item.description: High-level negotiations in Switzerland seeking a permanent end to the Iran war have ended, while technical talks will go on there for the rest of the week. US and Iranian delegation met for high-level talks following the recent signing of a "memorandum of understanding". However, the Iranian temporarily walked out after threats made by US President Donald Trump. Negotiators will seek to resolve some of the thornier issues between the US and Iran such as Tehran's nuclear programme and stockpiles of uranium.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: British PM Starmer announces resignation: What happens next?
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday said he was stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party and will leave office within weeks after growing pressure to hand over to a new leader who can try and revive the government’s flagging fortunes. Here's a look at the next steps in Britain's leadership contest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
